turns.user_message: They just brushed it off with a quick wave and told me not to make a big deal out of it, but the words have been echoing in my head ever since. I keep replaying that exact moment over and over, wondering why my chest feels so tight when it was supposed to be nothing. It has been lingering like this for weeks now, turning simple moments into heavy weights I carry around. I try to shake it off, but the quiet tension just settles deeper into my shoulders. I end up pacing around the room until my legs feel tired, hoping the feeling will finally fade away.

That same dismissive gesture keeps popping up in my thoughts, and it feels like it never actually left my mind. I catch myself holding my breath every time I remember how casually they moved on, while I am still stuck in the aftershock. It has been dragging on for months now, turning ordinary days into this exhausting loop of second-guessing everything I say. My hands get clammy and my thoughts start racing before I even realize what is happening. I just sit there staring at the wall, completely drained by how much energy it takes to pretend I am totally fine.

I cannot escape that quick wave and those careless words, because they have completely taken over my headspace and won't let me rest. Every single time I close my eyes, I am right back in that exact moment, feeling my heart hammer against my ribs like it wants to break out. It has been building up for so long that even a quiet room feels like it is closing in around me. My breathing gets shallow and my whole body tenses up until I am practically shaking from the inside out. I just pace back and forth endlessly, completely overwhelmed by a storm in my chest that refuses to settle down.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This has been going on for a while.
